mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months with rising PAYEMS, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above four percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates real discount rates and slows credit formation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds four percent, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and an increase in consumer prices signals stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate profit margins and equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in more than fifty-five percent of joint-up episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals imminent earnings downgrades and inventory destocking, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion periods, INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty-five percent of triggered instances, or the strategy Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues and profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns do not significantly exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below one million four hundred thousand units indicate tightening credit conditions and slowing construction activity that transmit into broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1400 episodes coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of instances, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and declining retail sales signals restrictive monetary policy successfully suppressing consumption, amplifying downside equity risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and RSAFS fails to generate a negative Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in more than fifty percent of joint-condition episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and an imminent consumption pullback, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in UNRATE are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of instances, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
